ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing A Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kathy Moon for (executor) JoAnn DeSantis.

The Court stated that there is a petition seeking various relief, that an accounting petition has now been filed with a hearing date of 6/27/16 which will take the status for that petition off calendar and asked what the expectation is for the other petition. Ms. Moon reported that there was an email sent to the Probate Examiner indicating that this petition did not need to remain on calendar. The Court took the status of filing a petition for final distribution and the petition for a citation of executor to appear to show cause, etc., off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For: Citation Of Executor To Appear To Show Cause (Status Report Or Petition For Final Distribution); Distribution Of Pecuniary Gift With Interest; An Accounting; Citation Of Executor To Appear And Show Cause Why She Should Not Be Removed And Letters Revoked; Fees And Costs; Other Relief As Appropriate
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For: Citation Of Executor To Appear To Show Cause (Status Report Or Petition For Final Distribution); Distribution Of Pecuniary Gift With Interest; An Accounting; Citation Of Executor To Appear And Show Cause Why She Should Not Be Removed And Letters Revoked; Fees And Costs; Other Relief As Appropriate
Off Calendar Apr-19-2016 Continued To May-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Shelton for (petitioner) Phalba Jeanes. Kathy Moon for (executor) Jo Ann DeSantis. Emilie Calhoun for (beneficiary) Simon Bye.

The Court stated that we are here on a petition seeking a citation and various orders, that it is not clear why it is not appropriate to set a status date to file the petition for final distribution and that the ex parte petition has been submitted. Ms. Moon stated that the petition seeks interest starting on 5/11/15, that per 12003 they are entitled to interest as of the date of death and that they have no dispute with waiting for the petition for final distribution. The Court stated that the interest issue will be addressed in the petition. Ms. Calhoun reported that she has tried contacting the executor's attorney many times between September and January, that there has been no response, that it is the executor's duty to respond to inquiries and that they do not disagree with the setting of a status date. The Court stated that it should be a straightforward final petition. The petition for citation of executor to appear to show cause, etc., was continued to May 24, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing a petition for final distribution is also set for that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For: Citation Of Executor To Appear To Show Cause (Status Report Or Petition For Final Distribution); Distribution Of Pecuniary Gift With Interest; An Accounting; Citation Of Executor To Appear And Show Cause Why She Should Not Be Removed And Letters Revoked; Fees And Costs; Other Relief As Appropriate
Off Calendar Apr-05-2016 Continued To Apr-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-11-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Confirming Estate Assets.
Off Calendar May-11-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Shelton & David Hornsby for Phalba Jeanes. Emilie Calhoun for (beneficiary) Simon Bye. Kathy Moon for (executor) Joanne Desantis.

The Court stated that this matter had been continued to give the parties time to see if they could work something out. Ms. Calhoun stated that she would like to submit on the pleadings and the other counsel agree. Mr. Hornsby stated that he would be happy to submit on the pleadings, that the only objection raised was lack of due execution, that there is no original will, that only one of the codicils is original, that both of the codicils are emails, that both codicils were signed, that Ms. Jeanes scanned the email and lost the original, explained the similarities between this case and the Estate of Button, that the clear intent was to dispose of the property upon the testators death and cited 6110(c)(2) regarding flaws and intent. Ms. Moon stated that the executor is unable to take sides. Ms. Calhoun stated that despite the information in the 3/6 email neither Ms. Jeanes nor the executor allowed Mr. Bye to come into the home to look for memorabilia, that there is no way to validate the email, that it would be easy to cut and paste an email, that the signatures on the emails are different, that Mr. Bye does not think they look like the decedent's signature, that they do not feel Ms. Jeanes has met the burden of proof, that 6110(c)(2) was meant for minor defects only and that in the 2/23 codicil the decedent says he has no energy to change the will. Mr. Hornsby stated that nowhere in the will does it say "this is not my will" and that the signatures were not disputed in any of the objections. Ms. Calhoun stated that the purported letter indicates that there are 4 blank checks which have not been produced. The Court stated that it is clear that the car was properly transferred pre-death, that it needs proposed orders from each side dealing with the car as well as orders regarding there views on the other issues. The petition for order confirming estate assets and the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary were taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Confirming Estate Assets.
Off Calendar Mar-03-2015 Continued To Apr-28-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Shelton for Phalba Jeanes. Kathy Moon for (executor) Joanne Desantis. Emilie Calhoun for (beneficiary) Simon Bye.

The court asked how the parties wished to proceed, if they want to mediate and if they have talked about the issues set out in the two letters. Mr. Shelton stated they have not talked to each other. Ms. Calhoun stated that they objected to the appointment of administrator because of how the estate was handled in the first months, there are trust issues and that they want the father's wishes followed out. Ms. Calhoun also stated that she will approach her client regarding settlement or an informal means of resolution, they have filed a will contest and that there is an issue as to what to do with the car as Mr. Horver is deceased. The petition for order confirming estate assets and the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary were continued to April 28, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to talk about resolution or consider mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-22-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun for Symon Bye. Kathy Moon for JoAnn DeSantis. Robert Ferris for Phalba Jeanes (Objector).

The Court stated that there are procedural problems with Mr. Bye's petition and that it does not know if there are sufficient grounds to not appoint the named Executor in the Will. Counsel referred to language in the Will and presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the issue of who should be appointed (Ms. DeSantis versus Elizabeth Adler), whether the Parties would serve as Co-Administrators, the issue of bond, Ms. DeSantis' prior actions regarding distribution of personal belongings, etc. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary appointing JoAnn DeSantis as Executor (subject to review of the order). Funds are to be deposited into a blocked account in lieu of bond. Mr. Bye's Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed was denied. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account was set for September 17, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Moon stipulated to filing her petition for instructions regarding the $100,000.00 check and the car no later than October 31, 2014. Status on filing of the petition for instructions was set for November 12, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Calhoun stated that it would be helpful to have an inventory of the personal tangible property.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-20-2014 Denied

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Continued To Aug-20-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kathy Moon for Joann Desantis. Emilie Calhoun for Simon Bye.

Ms. Calhoun stated that her Client maintains his position that Ms. Desantis is not competent to serve as Personal Representative, that her Client had no opportunity to respond to the reply brief, that he wants a neutral appointed and the opportunity to select a neutral, and raised the issue of the personal property being disposed of. Ms. Moon stated that the assets are all cash ($650,000.00) in one or two bank accounts, suggested placing the funds in a blocked account, spoke of a $100,000.00 check that was made out by the Decedent to Phalba Jeans dated March 6, 2014, and stated that her Client did a stop payment and told Ms. Jeans that it may be an asset of the Trust. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to August 20, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Wu. (Not Reported)
